_comment: Merge this into your Claude Code settings.json (project .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json). After each file write/edit, the risqlet CLI surfaces which tracked risks the change touches. Claude Code passes the tool payload as JSON on stdin, which `check --hook-input claude` reads directly — so this needs no shell and works on Linux, macOS, and Windows alike. It reports only and always exits 0; it never blocks an edit. Claude-Code-specific.
